content: Permission-aware RAG products (Glean, Onyx EE) mirror source ACLs into their own index rather than call source APIs at query time. Onyx offers two modes: auto-mirror external permissions per connector, or admin-assigned access (fewer external API calls). Oso analysis: three approaches - (1) query-time API filtering (simple, high latency, on critical path), (2) sync ACLs alongside data (fast local filtering; blocked when source lacks complete ACL APIs; group membership fragmented), (3) mirror permission logic with policy engine, syncing only authorization metadata (folders, groups, ownership chains). Identity resolution (mapping source user IDs/emails and groups to internal principals) is the universal prerequisite. Deny-by-default for unmapped principals is standard for enterprise trust.
